page.primary_keyword: marbella yacht charter hen party catering
page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter Hen Party Catering: Menu Ideas & Dietary Options

data.meta_description: Plan the perfect marbella yacht charter hen party catering with menu ideas, dietary options, and pricing from €749. Book your celebration today.
data.summary: Discover catering ideas and dietary options for a marbella yacht charter hen party, with flexible menus and all-inclusive packages starting at €749 for 2 hours.
key_facts: All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749/2h to €2,299/8h with drinks and snacks.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) from €4,719/4h include a free jet ski, drinks, and snacks.
Catering options range from light snacks to full platters; dietary needs catered on request.
All boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella, with easy access to local caterers.
Hen parties welcome on all 12+ pax boats like Ferretti 94, Canados 86, and Mangusta 80.
Custom menus for allergies, vegan, gluten-free, or halal diets available via WhatsApp.
No hidden costs: fuel, skipper, and taxes included in every booking.

faq.q: What catering is included in a marbella yacht charter hen party?
faq.a: Every charter includes light snacks, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. For hen parties, you can upgrade to full catering platters (e.g., seafood, charcuterie, tapas) via our WhatsApp booking—pricing on request.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food for a hen party on the yacht?
faq.a: Absolutely. You’re welcome to bring your own catering, cake, or decorations. Just let us know in advance via WhatsApp so we can coordinate storage and setup. No corkage fee applies.

faq.q: Do you cater for dietary restrictions like vegan or gluten-free?
faq.a: Yes. We work with local Marbella caterers to offer vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal options. Simply message us on WhatsApp with your requirements, and we’ll arrange a custom menu.

faq.q: What are the best boats for a hen party of 12 people?
faq.a: For 12 guests, we recommend the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), or Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). All depart from Puerto Banús and include drinks and snacks.

faq.q: How much does catering cost for a hen party yacht charter?
faq.a: Basic snacks and drinks are included in all charters. Upgraded platters (e.g., sushi, tapas, paella) are priced on request—typically €15–€30 per person depending on menu. Contact us via WhatsApp for a quote.

faq.q: Can I have a cake or decorations on board for a hen party?
faq.a: Yes, you’re welcome to bring a cake and decorations. Many hen parties bring banners, balloons, and party favours. Just confirm dimensions with us so we can ensure space on the boat.

faq.q: Do you offer halal or kosher catering options?
faq.a: We can arrange halal or kosher catering through our trusted local suppliers. Please notify us at least 48 hours before your charter via WhatsApp, and we’ll confirm availability and pricing.

faq.q: What is the minimum booking time for a hen party yacht charter?
faq.a: Most boats require a minimum of 2 hours for Tier A boats and 4 hours for Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80. For larger groups, we recommend 4–8 hours to enjoy the coast and swimming stops.

data.body_html:
Planning a marbella yacht charter hen party catering experience means balancing celebration with convenience. Whether you dream of a sunset cruise with cava and canapés or a full-day feast with paella and sangria, our fleet—departing from Puerto Banús—offers flexible menus to suit every bride tribe. All charters include drinks and snacks, but you can upgrade to custom platters for dietary needs like vegan, gluten-free, or halal. With boats like the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) starting at €4,719 for 4 hours (including a free jet ski), your hen party can focus on fun while we handle the food.

What’s Included in Every Marbella Yacht Charter Hen Party

Every booking—from the Boat Rental Marbella fleet—includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), and a welcome package of water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. For hen parties, this base package means you can arrive empty-handed and still toast the bride. The snacks typically include crisps, nuts, olives, and finger sandwiches—enough for a 2-hour cruise. For longer charters, we recommend upgrading to a full catering platter.

Catering Upgrade Options for Hen Parties

Our catering partners in Marbella offer a range of platters designed for groups. Popular choices include:

  • Tapas platter – jamón ibérico, manchego, patatas bravas, croquetas (€18/person)
  • Seafood spread – gambas, pulpo, mejillones, and calamares (€25/person)
  • Sushi box – mixed maki, nigiri, and edamame (€22/person)
  • Paella station – seafood or vegetarian paella served on board (€30/person, minimum 10 pax)

All upgrades are ordered via WhatsApp at least 48 hours before departure. Dietary restrictions? Just ask—we’ll tailor the menu.

Dietary Options: Vegan, Gluten-Free, Halal & More

We understand hen parties have diverse tastes. Our caterers can accommodate:

  • Vegan – plant-based tapas, vegan sushi, fresh fruit platters
  • Gluten-free – gluten-free bread, crackers, and dedicated platters
  • Halal – halal-certified meats and seafood, no alcohol (but we can offer non-alcoholic cava)
  • Dairy-free – lactose-free options for all courses
  • Nut-free – careful preparation to avoid cross-contamination

Simply message us on WhatsApp with your requirements, and we’ll confirm the menu and price. No extra charge for dietary swaps.

Best Boats for a Hen Party in Marbella

For a hen party of up to 12 guests, these boats offer spacious decks, sun pads, and shaded saloons:

BoatLengthPaxPrice from
Mangusta 80 (White/Grey)24m12€4,719/4h (incl. jet ski)
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12On request
Ferretti 9429m12On request
Canados 8626m12On request
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2h

For smaller groups (5–8 pax), the Bandido (11.5m, 8 pax) or Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) are great options. All depart from Puerto Banús.

How to Order Catering for Your Hen Party

Booking catering is simple. After you reserve your boat via our website or WhatsApp, let us know:

  1. Number of guests and any dietary restrictions
  2. Preferred menu style (tapas, seafood, paella, etc.)
  3. Any allergies or halal/kosher requirements
  4. Whether you want a cake or decorations

We’ll send a catering quote within 24 hours. Payment is via bank transfer or card. All food is delivered to the boat at Puerto Banús before departure.

Sample Hen Party Itinerary with Catering

A typical 4-hour charter for a hen party of 12 might look like this:

  • 14:00 – Board at Puerto Banús, welcome cava and tapas platter
  • 14:30 – Cruise along the Golden Mile towards Cabopino
  • 15:15 – Anchor at Cala del Faro for swimming and jet ski (on Mangusta 80)
  • 16:00 – Sushi box and sangria served on the aft deck
  • 16:45 – Return to Puerto Banús, cake cutting and photos
  • 18:00 – Disembark, ready for a night out

This itinerary is flexible—we can adjust timing, stops, and menu to your preferences.

Frequently asked questions

What catering is included in a marbella yacht charter hen party?

Every charter includes light snacks, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. For hen parties, you can upgrade to full catering platters (e.g., seafood, charcuterie, tapas) via our WhatsApp booking—pricing on request.

Can I bring my own food for a hen party on the yacht?

Absolutely. You’re welcome to bring your own catering, cake, or decorations. Just let us know in advance via WhatsApp so we can coordinate storage and setup. No corkage fee applies.

Do you cater for dietary restrictions like vegan or gluten-free?

Yes. We work with local Marbella caterers to offer vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal options. Simply message us on WhatsApp with your requirements, and we’ll arrange a custom menu.

What are the best boats for a hen party of 12 people?

For 12 guests, we recommend the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), or Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). All depart from Puerto Banús and include drinks and snacks.

How much does catering cost for a hen party yacht charter?

Basic snacks and drinks are included in all charters. Upgraded platters (e.g., sushi, tapas, paella) are priced on request—typically €15–€30 per person depending on menu. Contact us via WhatsApp for a quote.

Can I have a cake or decorations on board for a hen party?

Yes, you’re welcome to bring a cake and decorations. Many hen parties bring banners, balloons, and party favours. Just confirm dimensions with us so we can ensure space on the boat.

Do you offer halal or kosher catering options?

We can arrange halal or kosher catering through our trusted local suppliers. Please notify us at least 48 hours before your charter via WhatsApp, and we’ll confirm availability and pricing.

What is the minimum booking time for a hen party yacht charter?

Most boats require a minimum of 2 hours for Tier A boats and 4 hours for Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80. For larger groups, we recommend 4–8 hours to enjoy the coast and swimming stops.
